title: Gripping device of sewing thread for bookbinding sewing machine with gas jet
abstract: A gripping device (200) is proposed for gripping a sewing thread (415) in a bookbinding sewing machine (100). The gripping device (200) comprises a crochet (205) and a body (220) that are slidable reciprocally between an open condition, wherein a hook (215) of the crochet (205) hooks the sewing thread (415), and a closed condition, wherein the hook (215) blocks the sewing thread (415); the body (220) has one or more channels (270) crossing it for conveying a gas jet towards the hook (215). A bookbinding sewing machine (100) comprising one or more gripping devices (200) is also proposed. Moreover, a corresponding method for sewing signatures (115) into book blocks (155) is proposed.
claims:
Claims 1. A gripping device (200) for gripping a sewing thread (415) in a bookbinding sewing machine (100), the gripping device (200) comprising: a crochet (205) having a hook (215) for hooking the sewing thread (415), and a body (220) having an abutment wall for the hook (215), the body (220) and the crochet (205) being slidable reciprocally along a longitudinal direction of the crochet (205) between an open condition wherein the hook (215) is distal from the abutment wall of the body (220) for hooking the sewing thread (415) and a closed condition wherein the hook (215) is proximal to the abutment wall of the body (220) for blocking the sewing thread (415), characterized in that the body (220) has one or more inlets (275) for receiving a gas jet and one or more channels (270) crossing the body (220) between the inlets (275) and the abutment wall for conveying the gas jet towards the hook (215). 2. The gripping device (200) according to claim 1, wherein the gripping device (200) comprises biasing means (230) for biasing the body (220) and the crochet (205) to the closed condition at rest. 3. The gripping device (200) according to claim 2, wherein the crochet (205) is provided with a stop element (225), and wherein the biasing means (230) comprise elastic means (230) acting between the body (220) and the stop element (225) to push the body (220) and the crochet (205) away to each other at rest, the body (220) and the crochet (205) being slidable reciprocally towards the open condition in opposition to the elastic means (230). 4. The gripping device (200) according to claim 3, wherein the gripping device (200) comprises a further body (235) having a further stop element (245) for blocking the body (220) in abutment, the gripping device (200) being brought to the open condition in response to a push of the crochet (205) against the body (220) being blocked by the further stop element (245). 5. The gripping device (200) according to claim 4, wherein the further body (235) and an assembly (205,220,230) comprising the crochet (205), the body (220) and the elastic means (230) are slidable reciprocally between the closed condition and a safety condition wherein the further stop element (245) does not interfere with the 

body (220) for preventing the passage to the open condition. 6. The gripping device (200) according to claim 4 or 5, wherein the further body (235) comprises a sleeve (235) having the further stop element (245) at a first end thereof and a closure base (240) at a second end thereof and wherein the body (220) comprises a main portion and an interference element, an inner portion of the assembly (205,220,230) comprising the interference element of the body (220), the elastic means (230) and a corresponding portion of the crochet (205) comprising the stop element (225) being arranged between the further stop element (245) and the closure base (240), with the main portion of the body (220) projecting at least partly outside the sleeve (235) through the further stop element (245). 7. The gripping device (200) according to claim 6, wherein the sleeve (235) has a further inlet (260) at the first end thereof for receiving the gas jet, the sleeve (235) conveying the gas jet from the further inlet (260) to the inlets (275) of the body (220). 8. The gripping device (200) according to claim 6 or 7 when dependent on claim 5, wherein the closure base (240) has a through hole for the sliding of the crochet (205), the crochet (205) having a free portion opposite the hook (215) projecting outside the sleeve (235) through the through hole for moving the crochet (205) among the safety condition, the closed condition and the open condition. 9. The gripping device (200) according to any claim from 6 to 8 when dependent on claim 5, wherein a distance between the further stop element (245) and the closure base (240) is higher than a length of the internal portion of the assembly (205,220,230). 10. The gripping device (200) according to any claim from 6 to 9, wherein the further stop element (245) is arranged at a non-zero distance from the first end of the sleeve (235) thereby defining a corresponding cavity (250) for receiving the main portion of the body (220). 11. A bookbinding sewing machine (100) for sewing signatures (115) into book blocks (155), wherein the bookbinding sewing machine (100) comprises one or more gripping devices (200) according to any claim from 1 to 10 and blasting means (265) for providing the corresponding gas jet to each of the gripping devices (200). 12. The bookbinding sewing machine (100) according to claim 11, wherein the bookbinding sewing machine (100) comprises: 

 one or more needles (405) and one or more further crochets (410) for forming one or more stitchings (420,425) between the signatures (115) of each of the book blocks (155) by corresponding sewing threads (415), the stitchings (420,425) comprising one or more corresponding plain stitches (420) formed by the needles (405) and one or more corresponding looped stitches (425) formed by the crochets (410) concatenated to each other, the looped stitches (425) ending with corresponding free loops (435) on a last one of the signatures (415) of each completed one of the book blocks (155), one or more shuttles (440) corresponding to the sewing threads (415) each for bringing the corresponding sewing thread (415) downstream the corresponding plain stitches (420) along a formation direction thereof to an intermediate position aligned with the corresponding free loop (435) along an advancement direction (445) of the book blocks (155) during the sewing thereof, the gripping devices (200) corresponding to the sewing threads (415) each for bringing a closing portion of the corresponding sewing thread (415) from the intermediate position through the corresponding free loop (435) along the advancement direction (445), the closing portion consisting of a portion of the sewing thread (415) downstream the corresponding plain stitches (420) along the formation direction thereof, an actuation mechanism (255) for actuating the gripping devices (200) each to block the corresponding sewing thread (415) at a gripping point comprised in the closing portion thereof, one or more cutters (430) corresponding to the sewing threads (415) each for cutting the corresponding sewing thread (415) being blocked downstream the gripping point thereof along the formation direction of the plain stitches (420), a moving mechanism (255) for moving the gripping devices (200), each blocking the corresponding sewing thread (415) being brought through the corresponding free loop (435), to pull the sewing thread (415) being cut away from the free loop (435) thereby tightening the free loop (435) around the sewing thread (415), and the actuation mechanism (255) for bringing the gripping devices (200) each to the open condition thereby releasing the corresponding sewing thread (415) being 

pulled. 13. The bookbinding sewing machine (100) according to claim 11 or 12, wherein the bookbinding sewing machine (100) comprises means (305) for disabling each gripping device (200) individually. 14. The bookbinding sewing machine (100) according to claim 13, wherein the disabling means (305) comprise, for each gripping device (200), means (305) for keeping the hook (215) separated from the abutment wall of the body (220) when the gripping device is actuated to the closed condition. 15. The bookbinding sewing machine (100) according to claim 14, wherein the disabling means (305) comprise, for each gripping device (200), an eccentric (315) movable between a first position wherein it does not interfere with the crochet (205) and a second position wherein it interferes with the crochet (205) for spacing apart the hook (215) thereof from the abutment wall of the body (220). 16. A method for sewing signatures (115) into book blocks (155) in a bookbinding sewing machine (100), wherein the method comprises: forming, by one or more meddles (405) and one or more further crochets (410), one or more stitchings (420,425) between the signatures (115) of each of the book blocks (155) by corresponding sewing threads (415), the stitchings (420,425) comprising one or more corresponding plain stitches (420) formed by the needles (405) and one or more corresponding looped stitches (425) formed by the crochets (415) concatenated to each other, the looped stitches (425) ending with corresponding free loops (435) on a last one of the signatures (415) of each completed one of the book blocks (155), bringing, by corresponding one or more shuttles (440), the sewing threads (415) each downstream the corresponding plain stitches (420) along a formation direction thereof to an intermediate position aligned with the corresponding free loop (435) along an advancement direction (450) of the book blocks (155) during the sewing thereof, bringing, by one or more corresponding gripping devices (200) according to any claim from 1 to 10, a closing portion of each of the sewing threads (415) from the intermediate position through the corresponding free loop (435) along the advancement direction (445), the closing portion consisting of a portion of the sewing 

thread (415) downstream the corresponding plain stitches (420) along the formation direction thereof, actuating the gripping devices (200) each to block the corresponding sewing thread (415) at a gripping point comprised in the closing portion thereof, cutting the sewing threads (415) being blocked, by one or more corresponding cutters (430), each downstream the gripping point thereof along the formation direction of the plain stitches (420), moving the gripping devices (200), each blocking the corresponding sewing thread (415) being brought through the corresponding free loop (435), to pull the sewing thread (415) being cut away from the free loop (435) thereby tightening the free loop (435) around the sewing thread (415), bringing the gripping devices (200) each to the open condition thereby releasing the corresponding sewing thread (415) being pulled, and providing the corresponding gas jet to each of the gripping devices (200) in the open condition.
